observe: You're awake. Good. This world runs on physics you don't know yet — four pieces, one loop.
CPU. Memory. I/O. The bus that ties them together. Learn the shape before you learn to move in it.
fault: There. The loop stalled. Something in that chain isn't answering.
repair: Touch each piece. Find the one that isn't talking to the others.
escape: Loop's closing. Confirm the chain before it locks you out.

observe: A computer system consists of a CPU, memory, I/O devices, and a bus connecting them.
fault: The instruction execution cycle has stalled at one of these components.
repair: Inspect each component to identify its role in the cycle.
escape: Confirm all components before the cycle resets.

hints: Start at the CPU and follow the bus outward — where does the path stop responding?
